L. 103–182, § 611(1)(B)“(2) to present any forged, altered, or false document, paper, or manifest to a customs officer under without revealing the facts;section 1433(d) of this title“(3) to fail to make entry as required by section 1434, 1435, or 1644 of this title or , Appendix; or”.section 1509 of title 491986— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “Every master who fails to make the report or entry provided for in sections 1433, 1434, or 1435 of this title shall, for each offense, be liable to a fine of not more than $1,000 and, if the vessel have, or be discovered to have had, on board any merchandise (sea stores excepted), the importation of which into the United States is prohibited, or any spirits, wines, or other alcoholic liquors, such master shall be subject to an additional fine of not more than $2,000 or to imprisonment for not more than one year, or to both such fine and imprisonment.Pub. L. 99–570“Every master who presents a forged, altered, or false document or paper on making entry of a vessel as required by section 1434 or 1435 of this title, knowing the same to be forged, altered, or false and without revealing the fact, shall, in addition to any forfeiture to which in consequence the vessel may be subject, be liable to a fine of not more than $5,000 nor less than $50 or to imprisonment for not more than two years, or to both such fine and imprisonment.”1935—Act , inserted provisions relating to additional penalty for vessel carrying nonimportable merchandise or liquor and added second par.Aug. 5, 1935

children.text: to fail to comply with section 1431, 1433, or 1434 of this title or ;section 60105 of title 46

to present or transmit, electronically or otherwise, any forged, altered, or false document, paper, information, data or manifest to the Customs Service under section 1431, 1433(d), or 1434 of this title or without revealing the facts;section 60105 of title 46
to fail to make entry or to obtain clearance as required by section 1434 or 1644 of this title, , or section 1644a(b)(1) or (c)(1) of this title; orsection 60105 of title 46
to fail to comply with, or violate, any regulation prescribed under any section referred to in any of paragraphs (1) through (3).

children.heading: Civil penalty
children.label: b Civil penalty
children.text: Any master, person in charge of a vehicle, or aircraft pilot who commits any violation listed in subsection (a) is liable for a civil penalty of $5,000 for the first violation, and $10,000 for each subsequent violation, and any conveyance used in connection with any such violation is subject to seizure and forfeiture.

children.heading: Criminal penalty
children.label: c Criminal penalty
children.text: In addition to being liable for a civil penalty under subsection (b), any master, person in charge of a vehicle, or aircraft pilot who intentionally commits any violation listed in subsection (a) is, upon conviction, liable for a fine of not more than $2,000 or imprisonment for 1 year, or both; except that if the conveyance has, or is discovered to have had, on board any merchandise (other than sea stores or the equivalent for conveyances other than vessels) the importation of which into the United States is prohibited, such individual is liable for an additional fine of not more than $10,000 or imprisonment for not more than 5 years, or both.

children.heading: Additional civil penalty
children.label: d Additional civil penalty
children.text: If any merchandise (other than sea stores or the equivalent for conveyances other than a vessel) is imported or brought into the United States in or aboard a conveyance which was not properly reported or entered, the master, person in charge of a vehicle, or aircraft pilot shall be liable for a civil penalty equal to the value of the merchandise and the merchandise may be seized and forfeited unless properly entered by the importer or consignee. If the merchandise consists of any controlled substance listed in , the master, individual in charge of a vehicle, or pilot shall be liable to the penalties prescribed in that section.section 1584 of this title

children.heading: Civil penalties for postal shipments
children.label: e Civil penalties for postal shipments

children.heading: Civil penalty
children.label: 1 Civil penalty
children.text: A civil penalty shall be imposed against the United States Postal Service if the Postal Service accepts a shipment in violation of .section 1415(a)(3)(K)(vii)(I) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: has a low error rate in compliance with ;section 1415(a)(3)(K) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: is cooperating with U.S. Customs and Border Protection with respect to the violation of ; orsection 1415(a)(3)(K)(vii)(I) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: has taken remedial action to prevent future violations of .section 1415(a)(3)(K)(vii)(I) of this title

children.heading: Written notification
children.label: B Written notification
children.text: U.S. Customs and Border Protection shall issue a written notification to the Postal Service with respect to each exercise of the authority of subparagraph (A) to reduce or dismiss a civil penalty imposed pursuant to paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: has repeatedly committed violations of ,section 1415(a)(3)(K)(vii)(I) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: has failed to cooperate with U.S. Customs and Border Protection with respect to violations of , andsection 1415(a)(3)(K)(vii)(I) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: has an increasing error rate in compliance with ,section 1415(a)(3)(K) of this title
